user_stories.goal: I want to generate a personalised day-by-day itinerary by entering destination, trip length, budget level, and interests
user_stories.benefit: so that I get a ready-to-follow plan tailored to my constraints and preferences
acceptance_criteria: The system accepts inputs: destination (string), trip_length (integer >=1), budget (enum: low, medium, high), and interests (list of strings)
The returned itinerary contains exactly trip_length days
Each day lists at least one activity and each activity includes a name, suggested time/window, location, and a cost_estimate
Activities across the itinerary reflect the selected interests and show a budget tag consistent with the chosen budget level

user_stories.goal: I want to enter my trip details in a web form and view a clear day-by-day itinerary in the browser
user_stories.benefit: so that I can easily read and follow the plan without using developer tools or calling APIs
acceptance_criteria: The website provides a form with fields for destination, trip length, budget, and interests and validates inputs before submission
Submitting the form displays a human-readable itinerary page with day headings and ordered activities under each day
Each activity shown on the site includes time, location, brief description, and estimated cost
The site displays a total estimated budget summary and allows printing or saving the displayed itinerary as PDF

user_stories.role: As a developer integrating the service
user_stories.goal: I want a Flask API endpoint that returns the itinerary as JSON when given trip parameters
user_stories.benefit: so that other clients (mobile apps, third-party sites) can consume generated itineraries programmatically
acceptance_criteria: A POST /api/itinerary endpoint accepts JSON payload with keys: destination, trip_length, budget, interests
The endpoint responds with HTTP 200 and JSON containing a days array of length equal to trip_length
Each day object contains an ordered list of activity objects with fields: name, start_time (or time window), location, duration_estimate, cost_estimate, and tags
Invalid input returns a 4xx error with a helpful error message describing the validation failure

user_stories.role: As a user who liked an itinerary
user_stories.goal: I want to save and share an itinerary via a unique link
user_stories.benefit: so that I can revisit it later and send it to travel companions
acceptance_criteria: The UI provides a Save/Share action that generates a unique, shareable URL for the current itinerary
Visiting the URL loads the same itinerary in the browser or returns the same itinerary JSON via the API
Saved itineraries persist for at least 30 days and include a timestamp and the original input parameters (destination, trip_length, budget, interests)

user_stories.goal: I want to edit or swap suggested activities in the itinerary and have the system update time and cost totals
user_stories.benefit: so that the final plan matches my changing preferences and remains feasible within time and budget
acceptance_criteria: The UI allows replacing an activity with another suggested activity or adding/removing activities for a specific day
After an edit, the itinerary recalculates and displays updated per-day and total time and cost estimates
Edits can be saved back to the saved/shareable itinerary URL or exported via the API
